topic: Verify WIN-029 c4 self-patches — tldr_verdict math, H_eff alignment, Q-factor citations
our_argument_summary: WIN-029 (Schumann needs conductive ceiling, verdict: Self-Contradicted) carries two parallel arguments: (1) the correct spherical Schumann formula applied to any reasonable dome cavity recovers Earth radius (~6,366 km), not a dome height; (2) the dome publishes three mutually inconsistent cavity heights (9,572 / 9,086 / 8,537 km). c4 review flagged: (a) TLDR misattributed pairwise discrepancies to the spherical-formula argument (conflating the two arguments); (b) mixed-base convention on percentages (5.3% and 6.4% base=smaller, 10.8% base=larger); (c) H_eff inconsistency with WIN-002 (3,400 km / 22 Hz vs WIN-002 3,316 km / 22.6 Hz); (d) Q-factor citation missing; (e) averaging-convention choice unjustified. Decider self-patched (a)+(b)+(c)+(d). This verification confirms each patch and looks for residuals.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Schumann fundamental f₁ ≈ 7.83 Hz observed; spherical-cavity eigenfrequency f_n = c√(n(n+1))/(2πR) gives R ≈ 6,366 km. Dome quarter-wave formula H = c/(4f) gives H = 9,572 km. Dome firmament parameters: H₀ = 9,086 km (stated), H(r=0) = 8,537 km (from exponential fit). WIN-002 computes H_eff (linear radial average) = (H₀·L/R)(1−e^(−R/L)) ≈ 3,316 km → f = c/(4 × 3,316) = 22.6 Hz.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: WIN-029 monitor.py = hardcoded — no live frequency fetch; predicted=observed=7.83 Hz baked in.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Dome model retains both WIN-002 and WIN-029 by choosing different propagation speeds (v_a vs c) on the same circuit per WIN, without unifying dispersion relation.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py records Schumann 7.83 Hz as both expected and observed; no API call to a real Schumann monitoring source (HeartMath GCMS would be available but is not wired).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The dome substitutes a quarter-wave linear-waveguide formula for the standard spherical-cavity eigenmode formula — this is a DIFFERENT predicted value (9,572 km cavity height vs 6,366 km Earth radius), not a relabeling.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: 7.83 Hz Schumann fundamental was first measured by Balser & Wagner 1960; the dome adopts this measured value as its prediction in modern code (commit dates 2024+).
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: H = c/(4f) is a quarter-wave formula; the input is the (independently measured) frequency 7.83 Hz, not a dome parameter. The output 9,572 km is then claimed to match H₀ = 9,086 km — but H₀ is fitted, not derived. No path from disc_radius / firmament_height / sun_altitude to 9,572 km.

checks.check: tldr_verdict math corrected — pairwise discrepancies use base=smaller consistently
checks.evidence: Current tldr_verdict: "pairwise discrepancies of 5.3%, 6.4%, and 12.1%". Math verified: (9572−9086)/9086 = 5.35%; (9086−8537)/8537 = 6.43%; (9572−8537)/8537 = 12.12%. All three use base=smaller consistently. Old mixed-base 10.8% (which used base=larger) is no longer in tldr_verdict.

checks.check: tldr_verdict separates spherical-formula argument from mutual-inconsistency argument
checks.evidence: New text uses "Separately, the dome's three stated cavity heights ... are mutually inconsistent" to delimit the two distinct arguments. The pairwise discrepancies are no longer attributed to the spherical formula — they are the mutual-inconsistency observation.

checks.check: detail_evidence H_eff aligned to WIN-002 (3,316 km, 22.6 Hz)
checks.evidence: Current text: "The dome's own exponential cavity (H_eff ≈ 3,316 km linear radial average; see WIN-002) gives ~22.6 Hz." Cross-checked against WIN-002 detail_evidence which contains "H_eff = (H₀·L/R)(1−e^(−R/L)) ≈ 3,316 km → f = c/(4 × 3,316) = 22.6 Hz". Values now consistent across the two WINs.

checks.check: Q-factor citation added — Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002; Sátori et al. 2005
checks.evidence: Current text: "...narrow peaks at exact harmonics rather than the broad resonances observed (Nickolaenko & Hayakawa, 2002; Sátori et al., 2005)." Inline citation present immediately after the spectral-shape claim — matches citation density elsewhere in the WIN.

patch_verification.verdict: Three of three patched holes closed cleanly. Math is correct; cross-WIN consistency with WIN-002 restored; Q-factor citation present.

holes_found.description: tldr_EVIDENCE still contains the old broken framing AND broken math that the decider patched in tldr_VERDICT. Current tldr_evidence (unchanged): "Applying the correct spherical formula to the dome's own three stated cavity heights (9,572 km, 9,086 km, 8,537 km) gives discrepancies of 5.3%, 10.8%, and 6.4% respectively — no single dome geometry is self-consistent with the measurement." Two problems persist: (1) misattribution — the three percent figures are pairwise heights vs heights, not derived from "the correct spherical formula" (this is exactly the c4 hole 0 misattribution that was supposedly fixed); (2) mixed-base math — 10.8% only works as (9572−8537)/9572 (base=larger), while 5.3% and 6.4% use base=smaller (this is exactly the c4 hole 1 mixed-base finding). The decider patched tldr_verdict to use the new framing (5.3%, 6.4%, 12.1% with base=smaller) but tldr_evidence was missed. The result is a direct internal contradiction: TLDR-EVIDENCE says "5.3%, 10.8%, and 6.4%" while TLDR-VERDICT says "5.3%, 6.4%, and 12.1%" — same WIN, two TLDRs disagree on the third number. Per curmudgeon.md TLDR rules: "A TLDR that contradicts the evidence/verdict it summarizes is major." Defender screenshot risk: this is a one-page-load reproduction.
holes_found.recommendation: Apply the same patch shape to tldr_evidence as was applied to tldr_verdict. Recommended replacement for the broken sentence: "Separately, the dome's three stated cavity heights (9,572 km, 9,086 km, 8,537 km) are mutually inconsistent, with pairwise discrepancies of 5.3%, 6.4%, and 12.1% (base=smaller convention) — no single dome geometry satisfies all three." This restores math consistency with tldr_verdict and removes the spherical-formula misattribution.

holes_found.description: Carry-over from c4 hole 4 (still open): averaging-convention choice in detail_evidence not acknowledged. Current text uses "H_eff ≈ 3,316 km linear radial average" without noting that WIN-002 also computes an area-weighted average ("proper for a disc") giving H_eff ≈ 2,140 km → 35 Hz. A defender could argue "you cherry-picked the linear-radial average because it gives the worse-looking 22.6 Hz; the disc-appropriate area-weighted average gives 35 Hz which is also wrong but you should disclose you had a choice." The dome would still lose either way (both 22.6 Hz and 35 Hz are far from 7.83 Hz), but disclosure forecloses the cherry-pick attack.
holes_found.recommendation: In detail_evidence, after "H_eff ≈ 3,316 km linear radial average; see WIN-002", add parenthetical: "(the area-weighted average — proper for a disc per WIN-002 — gives H_eff ≈ 2,140 km → 35 Hz; either choice fails to match the observed 7.83 Hz)". This preempts the cherry-pick attack.

unresolved_prior_cycle.note: c4 hole 0 (TLDR misattribution) was patched in tldr_verdict but not in tldr_evidence. The same broken text persists in the sister TLDR field. No matching open-issue exists for the tldr_evidence twin; decider should create one or extend the existing patch.

c4 hole 1 (mixed-base math) was patched in tldr_verdict but not in tldr_evidence. The 10.8% figure (mixed-base) still appears alongside 5.3% and 6.4% in tldr_evidence. Same edit pattern as hole 0 above.
c4 hole 4 (averaging-convention disclosure) is a carry-over minor; decider did not address. Low priority but recommend bundling with the tldr_evidence fix.

stronger_arguments.description: The tldr_verdict rewrite is structurally cleaner than the tldr_evidence framing because it explicitly separates the two arguments (spherical-formula recovery of Earth radius, AND mutual inconsistency of dome heights). The tldr_evidence rewrite should mirror this structure: lead with the wrong-formula observation, then "separately" introduce the mutual-inconsistency observation. This makes the WIN harder to dismiss as a single point.

kernel_of_truth.description: Decider correctly identified the most defender-screenshot-risky breakage (tldr_verdict math/framing) and applied a high-quality rewrite (data/wins.json:029.tldr_verdict). H_eff/frequency alignment with WIN-002 is a meaningful cross-WIN consistency fix (data/wins.json:002.detail_evidence confirms 3,316 km / 22.6 Hz). Q-factor citation closes a c3 carry-over (data/wins.json:029.detail_evidence now cites Nickolaenko & Hayakawa, 2002; Sátori et al., 2005). The patch was high-quality where it landed.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The patch was incomplete — tldr_verdict and tldr_evidence are sister fields that should mirror each other. Patching only the verdict side and leaving the evidence side with the broken framing AND broken math creates a NEW defender-screenshot risk: an internal TLDR-vs-TLDR contradiction. The defender does not need to dig — they screenshot the WIN panel and the two TLDRs disagree on the third pairwise percentage.

deeper_analysis: The decider patches correctly addressed three of the four substantive c4 holes (the major TLDR misattribution + the moderate mixed-base math, the minor H_eff cross-WIN alignment, the minor Q-factor citation). The patch quality on tldr_verdict is good — the rewrite is cleaner than the original, explicitly separates the two parallel arguments, and uses consistent base=smaller math. However, the patch only touched the verdict-side TLDR. The evidence-side TLDR still carries the c4-hole-0 misattribution AND the c4-hole-1 mixed-base math AND now creates a NEW intra-WIN contradiction (the two TLDRs disagree on the third pairwise percentage). The fix is a near-copy of the verdict-side rewrite applied to evidence-side prose. Also unaddressed: c4 hole 4 (averaging-convention disclosure) — a minor preemption against cherry-pick attacks. Both findings carry forward to c5.

citations_verified: Nickolaenko & Hayakawa, 2002 (Q-factor — verified present in detail_evidence)
Sátori et al., 2005 (Q-factor — verified present in detail_evidence)
WIN-002 cross-WIN claim (3,316 km / 22.6 Hz — verified present in WIN-002 detail_evidence)
citations_unchecked: Sentman 1990 (cited but DOI not checked in this review)
Mushtak & Williams 2002 (cited but DOI not checked)

summary_for_decider: Three of three patched holes closed cleanly: tldr_verdict math/framing fix, detail_evidence H_eff alignment to WIN-002, detail_evidence Q-factor citations. RESIDUAL MAJOR HOLE: tldr_evidence still has the OLD broken framing (spherical-formula misattribution) AND OLD broken math (mixed-base 5.3%/10.8%/6.4%) that the decider fixed in tldr_verdict. Result: TLDR-verdict and TLDR-evidence now CONTRADICT each other on the third pairwise percentage (12.1% vs 10.8%). Recommend applying the same rewrite to tldr_evidence. Minor carry-over: averaging-convention disclosure in detail_evidence (c4 hole 4). Both are quick edits.
